Components</h1>

<h2 style="font-weight: 400">What is this?</h2>

`@mc-made/web-components` is a future-ready library of
[web components](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Web_Components)
built on Made's design language. Developed as a universal component library for
the web, Made Web Components are feature rich, offer seamless interoperability,
accessibility and themeability built in.

---

<h2 style="font-weight: 400">What are Web components?</h2>

They are a suite of technologies built into the browser that allows us to build
reusable HTML elements with encapsulated styles and behavior. They work across
different frameworks, like Angular, React and Vue, as well as web environments
such as Eleventy and Wordpress. Many of the components in this library are
drop-in replacements for native browser elements like `<button>` and `<input>`.

---

<h2 style="font-weight: 400">Quick Start</h2>

**Note: Check out our [Getting Started](./docs/getting-started.mdx) guide for
our comprehensive docs.**

Install Made Web Components and Made Design Tokens using npm:

```
npm install @mc-made/web-components @mc-made/design-tokens
```

Once installed make sure the source files are available to your app. One way to
do this is to create a route in your app called /mwc, for instance, that serves
static files from node_modules/@mc-made.

Once you’ve done that then add the following tags to your page. Make sure to
match your route of choice:

```
<link rel="stylesheet" href="/mwc/design-tokens/themes/mastercard/light.js" />
<script type="module" src="/mwc/web-components/dist/bundle.js"></script>
```

You now have access to all Made Web Components. Add a button to you page using:

```
<made-button>Click me</made-button>
```

If you are using a bundler see the Getting Started guide.

---

<h2 style="font-weight: 400">Browser Support</h2>

Made Web Components are tested in the latest two versions of the following
browsers at the time of each release. If you need to support IE11 or
pre-Chromium Edge, these components are not for you.

<br />
<p>
    <img src="./public/chrome.png" alt="Chrome" width="64" height="64" />
    <img src="./public/safari.png" alt="Safari" width="64" height="64" />
    <img src="./public/firefox.png" alt="Firefox" width="64" height="64" />
    <img src="./public/edge.png" alt="Edge" width="64" height="64" />
    <img src="./public/opera.png" alt="Opera" width="64" height="64" />
</p>
